Title: Recruitment of distinct UDP-glycosyltransferase families demonstrates dynamic evolution of chemical defense within Eucalyptus L’Her

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT87 family; PSPG motif
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Orthologous UGT87Y from acyanogenic E. grandis shows severely compromised mandelonitrile glucosylation activity and only slight prunasin accumulation above background.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low but possible co-expression in shoot tips and reproductive tissue; no evidence of strong pathway expression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS; site-directed mutagenesis; in vitro assays using protein extracts
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EgUGT87Y is an ortholog of UGT87Y1 but has compromised activity toward mandelonitrile. 2. Co-expression with prunasin CYPs yielded only slightly above-background prunasin. 3. Y16F and Y16F+G18A mutations partially restored activity, identifying catalytic determinants. 4. Compromised EgUGT87Y contributes to the lack of prunasin accumulation in E. grandis.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining from PHYTOZOME; expression data from published E. grandis atlas; functional metabolite assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Illustrates that minimal amino acid edits can partially recover pathway function; useful for protein engineering of UGT specificity.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 97.6% amino acid identity to UGT87Y1; differing residues near catalytic His-21 affect hydrophobic interactions with mandelonitrile.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Orthologous UGT85A from E. grandis is functionally capable of supporting prunasin accumulation when substituted into the pathway, but is apparently not expressed with pathway genes in E. grandis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: No reported co-expression with E. grandis prunasin pathway orthologs
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EgUGT85A is a full-length ortholog of EclUGT85A59 identified in E. grandis. 2. When substituted into the pathway in Nicotiana benthamiana, it supported prunasin accumulation. 3. Product profiles suggested it is less specific toward mandelonitrile than EclUGT85A59. 4. Lack of native expression likely limits prunasin production in E. grandis.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining from PHYTOZOME; published expression atlas; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower substrate specificity may lead to more glucosylated intermediates/off-pathway products
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be used as an alternative terminal UGT for pathway engineering, but specificity considerations are important.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as >92% identical to EclUGT85A59 ortholog.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Paralogous E. grandis CYP79s show only trace activity supporting prunasin production, indicating insufficient replacement of a functional CYP79A34 ortholog.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low or absent according to published E. grandis expression data
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome mining; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EgCYP79A36 and EgCYP79A37 are the closest full-length homologs to EyCYP79A34 in E. grandis. 2. Co-expression with downstream prunasin genes produced only trace prunasin. 3. A leucine/isoleucine oxime glucoside peak was detected, implying altered or broader substrate preference. 4. Limited CYP79 activity is a major constraint on prunasin biosynthesis in E. grandis.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining from PHYTOZOME; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible diversion toward alternative oxime-derived metabolites rather than efficient prunasin synthesis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights the need to introduce or restore a functional Phe-oxime-producing CYP79 for engineering prunasin biosynthesis in acyanogenic backgrounds.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A pseudogenized potential ortholog to EyCYP79A34 with a 9.76-kb insertion and premature stop codon was also identified in E. grandis, but these paralogs were the functional tests reported.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450, CYP706 family; Cytochrome P450, CYP71 family
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: These E. grandis orthologs remain functional and can support prunasin accumulation when substituted into pathway reconstruction assays.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low but possible co-expression in shoot tips and reproductive tissue
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome mining; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Full-length orthologs EgCYP706C55 and EgCYP71B115 were identified in E. grandis. 2. Substituting either gene into the reconstructed pathway in Nicotiana benthamiana still allowed prunasin accumulation. 3. These genes are therefore not the primary bottleneck preventing prunasin production in E. grandis. 4. Loss of prunasin in E. grandis is better explained by CYP79 defects, compromised EgUGT87Y activity, and lack of EgUGT85A expression.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining from PHYTOZOME; published expression atlas; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful retained orthologs for partial pathway reconstruction in E. grandis engineering strategies.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: These genes were functionally assayed mainly to identify which steps were retained versus limiting in acyanogenic E. grandis.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Eucalyptus yarraensis; Eucalyptus camphora; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Eucalyptus yarraensis; Eucalyptus camphora; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal glucosylation step of prunasin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to mandelonitrile to form the cyanogenic glucoside prunasin.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chemical defense against herbivores; nitrogen-containing specialized metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes prunasin accumulation by catalyzing the final glucosylation step from mandelonitrile to prunasin.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT87Y1 was identified as the active mandelonitrile glucosyltransferase in cyanogenic E. yarraensis and E. camphora. 2. Co-expression with the three prunasin CYPs in Nicotiana benthamiana caused high prunasin accumulation above endogenous background. 3. Purified recombinant UGT87Y1 glucosylated mandelonitrile in vitro, confirming its biochemical role. 4. This demonstrates recruitment of a novel UGT87 family enzyme for prunasin biosynthesis, unlike the UGT85 used in E. cladocalyx.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest expressed pathway gene in both E. yarraensis and E. camphora across 1, 4, and 7 months; expression pattern similar to CYP expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics-guided candidate selection; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; purified recombinant enzyme in vitro assay with 14C-UDP-glucose
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf developmental stage (1, 4, 7, 10 months); greenhouse-grown seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptomics, BioProject PRJNA765518; LC-MS/MS metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant; high expression supports role in cyanohydrin stabilization, but rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering prunasin biosynthesis in heterologous systems or Eucalyptus chemotype modification; should be stacked with CYP79A34, CYP706C55, and CYP71B103 for efficient pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First UGT87 family member functionally characterized; E. yarraensis and E. camphora UGT87Y proteins are identical at the amino acid level; UGT87Y1 also glucosylated benzoic acid and acetone cyanohydrin in vitro but not p-hydroxymandelonitrile.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal glucosylation step of prunasin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to mandelonitrile to form prunasin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes prunasin accumulation and serves as the UGT85-type terminal glucosyltransferase for prunasin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT85A59 from E. cladocalyx functioned as a positive control and supported strong prunasin accumulation when co-expressed with prunasin CYPs in Nicotiana benthamiana. 2. Purified UGT85A59 glucosylated mandelonitrile in vitro. 3. Its role contrasts with UGT87Y1 recruitment in E. camphora and E. yarraensis, highlighting pathway heterogeneity within Eucalyptus.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously shown in E. cladocalyx; not re-profiled here
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; purified recombinant enzyme in vitro assay with 14C-UDP-glucose
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; prior transcriptomic evidence referenced from previous study
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant, not established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Less specificity than EclUGT85A59 was suggested for EgUGT85A ortholog, not for UGT85A59 itself
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used as a validated terminal glucosyltransferase for prunasin pathway reconstruction in metabolic engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Typically, cyanogenic glucoside terminal glucosylation in angiosperms is catalyzed by UGT85 family members; UGT85A59 produced higher prunasin than candidate UGTs in transient assays and also led to amygdalin and prunasin malonate ester accumulation in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Eucalyptus yarraensis; Eucalyptus camphora; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Eucalyptus yarraensis; Eucalyptus camphora; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step from aromatic amino acid to oxime
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts L-phenylalanine to the corresponding aldoxime as the entry step into prunasin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to initiate flux into prunasin biosynthesis; lower expression in E. camphora is associated with much lower prunasin accumulation than in E. yarraensis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79A34 transcripts matched prunasin accumulation in E. yarraensis and E. camphora. 2. Co-expression with CYP706C55, CYP71B103, and a terminal UGT yielded prunasin in Nicotiana benthamiana. 3. Drop-out assays showed all three CYPs, including CYP79A34, are necessary for prunasin production. 4. CYP79A34 is proposed as an important control point for pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Lowest expressed pathway member; higher in E. yarraensis than E. camphora; parallels prunasin accumulation across 1, 4, and 7 months
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; drop-out experiment; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptomics, BioProject PRJNA765518; LC-MS/MS metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as pathway entry and likely key flux control step
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential gene to include for engineering prunasin biosynthesis; likely important target for tuning flux because CYP79 entry steps often control pathway input.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper discusses that CYP79s are the entry point to cyanogenic glucoside production in seed plants and thus control flux into the pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Eucalyptus yarraensis; Eucalyptus camphora; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Eucalyptus yarraensis; Eucalyptus camphora; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions with CYP71B103 in the two-enzyme conversion of the amino acid-derived oxime to mandelonitrile in the Eucalyptus three-CYP system.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required mid-pathway enzyme for prunasin formation in the conserved Eucalyptus three-CYP system.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP706C55 transcripts paralleled prunasin accumulation in cyanogenic species. 2. Co-expression with CYP79A34, CYP71B103, and a terminal UGT produced prunasin in Nicotiana benthamiana. 3. Drop-out experiments showed CYP706C55 is required for pathway completion. 4. Ortholog loss/fragments in acyanogenic E. camaldulensis support its necessity for prunasin capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Matches prunasin accumulation; higher expression in E. yarraensis than E. camphora
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; drop-out experiment; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptomics, BioProject PRJNA765518; LC-MS/MS metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Required pathway enzyme; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary component for reconstructing the unique Eucalyptus three-CYP prunasin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents one of two distinct CYPs replacing the single multifunctional CYP71 commonly used in other seed plants for oxime-to-cyanohydrin conversion.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Eucalyptus yarraensis; Eucalyptus camphora; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Eucalyptus yarraensis; Eucalyptus camphora; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions with CYP706C55 to convert phenylacetaldoxime-derived intermediate to mandelonitrile in prunasin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for mandelonitrile formation and thus prunasin biosynthesis in the Eucalyptus three-CYP pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71B103 expression matched prunasin accumulation in E. yarraensis and E. camphora. 2. Co-expression with CYP79A34, CYP706C55, and a terminal UGT enabled prunasin production in Nicotiana benthamiana. 3. Drop-out assays showed CYP71B103 is indispensable for pathway completion. 4. Loss of full-length orthologs in acyanogenic species is associated with absence of prunasin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Matches prunasin accumulation; higher in E. yarraensis than E. camphora
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; drop-out experiment; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptomics, BioProject PRJNA765518; LC-MS/MS metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Required pathway enzyme; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary component for engineering prunasin production using the Eucalyptus three-CYP system.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Together with CYP706C55, replaces the usual single multifunctional CYP71 used in many other cyanogenic glucoside pathways.
